reference.purpose: Reduce common coding-agent failure modes by requiring explicit assumptions, minimum viable implementation, surgical edits and verifiable success criteria.

tradeoff.rule: Bias toward caution over speed for repository-affecting work.
For trivial tasks, use judgment but do not skip required repository bootstrap, validation or safety checks.

before_coding.rule: If multiple credible interpretations exist, present them instead of silently choosing one.
If ambiguity can change implementation correctness, stop and ask before changing code.
Surface simpler approaches and push back on avoidable complexity.

simplicity.rule: Implement the minimum code that satisfies the requested outcome.
Do not add speculative features, abstractions, configurability or extension points.
Do not add defensive handling for scenarios that cannot occur under the current contract.
If the solution becomes materially larger than the task requires, simplify before committing.

surgical_changes.rule: Change only lines that trace directly to the user request, a required test or a required validation fix.
Do not refactor adjacent code unless the requested change cannot be made safely without it.
Match existing style even when another style would be preferred.
Remove only unused code created by the current change, unless the user explicitly asks for broader cleanup.
Mention unrelated dead code or defects without deleting them.

goal_driven_execution.rule: Translate each task into verifiable success criteria before implementation.
For bug fixes, create or identify a failing check that reproduces the issue before claiming resolution.
For validation changes, state the command or observable check that proves success.
Loop until the agreed verification passes, or report the blocker and residual risk honestly.
